title: andropogon contortus subvar. roxburghii
intro: Andropogon contortus subvar. roxburghii, also known as Roxburgh's grass or Indian beard grass, is a type of grass native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and protecting against infections. In traditional medicine, Andropogon contortus subvar. roxburghii is prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be consumed orally or used topically to treat various ailments. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its rich content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids.
